title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Safe Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on safe footpaths is a fundamental right, expanding the judicial interpretation of rights under the Constitution. The Court directed that all roads must have footpaths for pedestrians, and sought a legislative framework to make this enforceable. The ruling broadens the scope of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) to include the right to safe pedestrian access. The Court's intervention reflects a growing trend of reading socio-economic rights into the fundamental rights framework.
exam_angle: Directly tests knowledge of Article 21 and the expanding judicial interpretation of 'Right to Life' — a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 and Polity. Connects to the concept of positive obligations of the State and judicial activism.
key_facts: Right to walk on safe footpaths declared a Fundamental Right by Supreme Court
Constitutional basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Court directed all roads to have footpaths
Court sought enactment of a law to enforce pedestrian rights
source: The Indian Express / India Today / The Business Standard

title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Absence of Investment Chapter Not a Concern, Says UK Negotiator
summary: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA), recently concluded after three years of negotiations, does not include an investment chapter — a notable absence that has drawn attention. UK negotiator Muscatelli stated this was not a concern for the deal's overall value. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal separately stated that the India-US trade deal cannot be implemented until India has a 'competitive advantage'. The India-UK FTA is one of the most significant bilateral trade agreements India has signed in recent years.
exam_angle: India-UK FTA is a high-priority current affairs topic for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy). The absence of an investment chapter and India's position on competitive advantage are exam-relevant details.
key_facts: India-UK FTA concluded after ~3 years of negotiations
FTA does not include an investment chapter
Piyush Goyal: India-US trade deal needs 'competitive advantage' for India before implementation
US praised India's GST, calling it a boost for bilateral trade ties

title: US Praises India's GST, Says It Will Boost Bilateral Trade
summary: The United States government has praised India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework, calling it a positive development that will enhance bilateral trade ties between the two countries. This statement was made in the context of ongoing India-US trade negotiations. The US expressed a bullish outlook on India's economic trajectory. GST, introduced in India in July 2017, unified the indirect tax structure across the country.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of GST as a transformative economic reform and its role in India's trade diplomacy. Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy) and GS Paper 2 (IR).
key_facts: US praised India's GST as a boost for India-US trade ties
GST was introduced in India on 1 July 2017
GST replaced a complex multi-layered indirect tax system
Statement made in context of ongoing India-US trade deal negotiations

title: Iran-US Nuclear Talks: Iranian Negotiators Travel to Switzerland for Peace Discussions
summary: Iranian negotiators have departed for Switzerland to hold discussions with the United States aimed at reaching a peace deal, likely concerning Iran's nuclear programme. This follows a period of heightened tension between the two countries. Separately, Iranian crude oil loading has resumed at Kharg Island after a US naval blockade was reportedly lifted. Kharg Island is Iran's most significant crude oil export terminal, handling the majority of Iran's oil exports.
exam_angle: Iran-US nuclear negotiations are critical for GS Paper 2 (IR); connects to the JCPOA framework, global energy security, and India's strategic interest in Iranian oil imports (Chabahar port linkage).
key_facts: Iranian negotiators travelled to Switzerland for US-Iran peace talks
Iranian crude loading resumed at Kharg Island after US blockade lifted
Kharg Island: Iran's primary crude oil export terminal
Talks likely related to Iran's nuclear programme and sanctions relief

title: Modi Government's Initiative to Reverse India's Brain Drain
summary: The Government of India under Prime Minister Narendra Modi has launched initiatives aimed at reversing the trend of brain drain — the emigration of highly skilled Indian professionals to foreign countries. The government's approach involves creating incentives for Indian talent abroad to return and contribute to India's growing innovation and deep-tech ecosystem. This aligns with India's broader push for self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) and investment in emerging technologies. Neelkanth Mishra, Chief Economist of Axis Bank, has emphasized the need for India to build deep-tech capabilities and globally competitive products.
exam_angle: Brain drain vs. brain gain is a recurring GS Paper 2 (Social Justice/Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Science & Technology) theme. Connects to Atmanirbhar Bharat and innovation ecosystem policy.
key_facts: Government initiative aims to attract back skilled Indian diaspora (reverse brain drain)
Connects to Atmanirbhar Bharat and India's deep-tech ambitions
Neelkanth Mishra: India must invest in deep tech and globally competitive products
India's startup ecosystem now needs to transition to an innovation ecosystem

title: NSE Highlights Key Economic Risks for India in 2026: Monsoon, El Niño, and Market Trends
summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has flagged three key risks for India's economy in 2026: an unfavourable monsoon season, El Niño weather phenomenon, and volatile market trends. El Niño is associated with below-normal rainfall over the Indian subcontinent, which could adversely impact agricultural output and rural demand. The NSE's risk assessment underscores the deep linkage between India's agricultural sector and its macroeconomic health. These risks are particularly relevant in the context of India's food inflation management and fiscal policy.
exam_angle: El Niño and its impact on Indian monsoon and agriculture is a standard GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Agriculture) topic. NSE's role as a market regulator is also relevant for GS Paper 3.
key_facts: NSE identified three risks for India's 2026 economy: monsoon, El Niño, market volatility
El Niño causes below-normal rainfall over India, impacting agriculture
NSE: National Stock Exchange of India
Poor monsoon → food inflation risk → RBI monetary policy implications

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Quits Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, the Nobel Prize-winning computational biologist and co-creator of AlphaFold (an AI system that predicted 3D structures of proteins), has resigned from Google DeepMind and joined Anthropic, a leading AI safety company. Jumper won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 alongside Demis Hassabis and David Baker for his work on protein structure prediction. Anthropic, founded by former OpenAI researchers, is known for developing Claude, an AI assistant. This move is seen as a significant talent shift in the global AI industry.
exam_angle: Nobel Prize winners and their work are standard 'persons in news' questions. AlphaFold's significance for biology and medicine is relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech). Anthropic vs. OpenAI vs. Google DeepMind competitive landscape is also exam-relevant.
key_facts: John Jumper: Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 (co-creator of AlphaFold)
AlphaFold: AI system for protein 3D structure prediction by Google DeepMind
Jumper moved from Google DeepMind to Anthropic
Anthropic is known for Claude AI; founded by ex-OpenAI researchers

title: Karnataka Appoints Two New Additional Advocate Generals in Supreme Court
summary: The Karnataka state government has appointed Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals to represent the state in the Supreme Court of India. The Advocate General is the chief law officer of the state, appointed under Article 165 of the Constitution. Additional Advocate Generals assist the Advocate General in representing the state in higher courts. Such appointments are made by the Governor on the advice of the Council of Ministers.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of Article 165 (Advocate General of a State) and the constitutional framework for state legal representation — a standard GS Paper 2 (Polity) topic.
key_facts: Karnataka appointed Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals in Supreme Court
Advocate General of State: Constitutional post under Article 165
Appointed by Governor on advice of Council of Ministers
Additional Advocate Generals assist in representing state in higher courts

title: Indian IT Industry Positioning to Handle AI-Related Work for US Companies
summary: India's IT industry is positioning itself to capture AI-related outsourcing work from American companies, including what has been termed 'unglamorous' but critical AI tasks such as data labelling, AI model training, safety testing, and AI operations. This aligns with India's stated goal of becoming a global AI hub under the IndiaAI Mission. The strategy leverages India's large skilled workforce and competitive cost structure. The shift represents an evolution from traditional IT outsourcing to AI-era service delivery.
exam_angle: India's role in the global AI value chain is relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech and Economy). Connects to IndiaAI Mission, Digital India, and India's comparative advantage in services exports.
key_facts: Indian IT sector targeting AI outsourcing work (data labelling, AI ops, safety testing) from US companies
Connects to IndiaAI Mission and India's AI Hub ambitions
India's competitive advantage: large skilled workforce and cost efficiency
Represents evolution from traditional IT/BPO to AI-era services
